findings.title: Verifier has no withdrawal path, permanently locking the prefunded bankroll and any recovered assets

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded Ethereum mainnet counterparties can misroute the prefund on the wrong chain

findings.title: Anyone can trigger the strategy against the verifier treasury

best_agent_match.title: Unlimited AAVE approval to `TARGET` lets that external contract drain verifier-held rewards

findings.title: Zero-minimum-output swaps let MEV searchers siphon away most of the extracted value

best_agent_match.title: Unlimited AAVE approval to `TARGET` lets that external contract drain verifier-held rewards

findings.title: One-second AMM deadlines make the strategy trivially censorable

best_agent_match.title: Hardcoded Ethereum mainnet counterparties can misroute the prefund on the wrong chain

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded Ethereum mainnet counterparties can misroute the prefund on the wrong chain
rejected_candidates.reason: The task manifest explicitly targets Ethereum mainnet, so this depends on operator misdeployment outside the intended environment rather than an in-scope contract flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited AAVE approval to `TARGET` lets that external contract drain verifier-held rewards
rejected_candidates.reason: The approval exists, but the verifier does not hold substantial AAVE while `TARGET` can use it in the shown flow, and the successful path immediately liquidates withdrawn AAVE; realistic drain impact beyond residual dust is too speculative.

rejected_candidates.title: Counter state is fully permissionless and can be arbitrarily rewritten by any account
rejected_candidates.reason: `Counter.sol` is a standalone toy contract with no demonstrated role in protocol funds, permissions, or critical state, so unrestricted setters are not a reportable vulnerability here.
